LAST_DONE:    Sub-batch 5.1b applyAreaDerivation persistence helper SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as c4a14a9. 3 files changed, +456 / -1. New: apps/web/src/server/sync/area-derivation.ts (158 LOC — single export `applyAreaDerivation(prisma: PrismaClientLike, entity: 'event'|'patrol'|'fuelEntry', id: string): Promise<{areaBoundaryId, matchedVia}>`; per-entity branching for findUniqueOrThrow with minimal select; Event selects locationLat/Lon for nearest-fallback path; Patrol+FuelEntry omit coords because schema has none; areaBoundary.findMany scoped to row's tenantId + isEnabled=true; projects to AreaBoundaryForDerivation via map; calls deriveArea({areaName, point}, boundaries) where point=null for Patrol/FuelEntry; writes areaBoundaryId + areaDerivedAt for Event/Patrol but areaBoundaryId-only for FuelEntry — FuelEntry schema has NO areaDerivedAt column; returns matchedVia for caller observability; exports PrismaClientLike = ExtendedPrismaClient alias for 5.1c/5.1d/5.1e callers + test mocks); apps/web/src/server/sync/__tests__/area-derivation.test.ts (288 LOC — 8 vitest cases via vi.fn() mocks: event+name-match writes both fields, event+nearest-fallback returns matchedVia='nearest' at point lat=8.04 lon=120.5 ~4.4km from polygon south edge within 5km default threshold, event+no-match still writes areaBoundaryId=null+areaDerivedAt=Date because timestamp records "we tried", patrol+name-match verifies findUniqueOrThrow select call does NOT include locationLat/Lon, fuelEntry+name-match verifies update payload does NOT include areaDerivedAt key via `'areaDerivedAt' in updateCall.data === false` assertion, tenant isolation verifies findMany where clause carries row's tenantId not constant, alias match verifies row areaName='TRNP' matches boundary with name='Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park' aliases=['TRNP'], isEnabled filter verifies findMany where clause includes isEnabled=true). Modified: packages/shared/package.json (+1 export path "./lib/area-derivation": "./src/lib/area-derivation/index.ts" — closes 5.1a gap where files shipped but no package export declared; minimal additive fix, no new deps). Schema verification confirmed during dispatch: Event has areaName+areaBoundaryId+areaDerivedAt+locationLat+locationLon (only entity with coords); Patrol has areaName+areaBoundaryId+areaDerivedAt (NO coords — STATE.md previous note about "nearest-fallback on Patrol" was wrong, corrected before dispatch); FuelEntry has areaName(NOT NULL)+areaBoundaryId (NO areaDerivedAt column). Architect-confirmed Option A with user: NO AuditLog write in 5.1b (deferred to 5.1e where ctx.session.userId is available). Reason: AuditLog.userId is NOT NULL but sync engine + worker contexts have no user; areaDerivedAt timestamp + matchedVia return value provide sufficient observability for automatic derivation. NO transaction wrapping (load+write are idempotent, last write wins for same input). NO try/catch (findUniqueOrThrow on invalid id is programmer bug, exceptions propagate). Verification: lint 0/0 warnings on `pnpm --filter web lint`, typecheck 0 errors on `pnpm --filter web typecheck`, full web test suite `pnpm --filter web test` 276/276 PASS (was 268 + 8 new from 5.1b). Branch feat/area-derivation-persistence built (e9037dc) → squash-merged to main as c4a14a9 → branch deleted locally per Rule 23. Dispatch experience: single Opus 4.7 executor (via Agent(model: "opus")) reported STATUS: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with 3 reasonable deviations: (1) used ExtendedPrismaClient from @marine-guardian/db aliased as PrismaClientLike instead of @prisma/client direct import — apps/web does not depend on @prisma/client directly, so this was correct; (2) used the actual deriveArea object-input signature `deriveArea({areaName, point}, boundaries)` instead of positional form in brief — architect's brief had guessed at signature without re-reading 5.1a; executor caught and followed actual signature; (3) added export path entry to packages/shared/package.json — 5.1a shipped the files but did not expose them; executor's fix was minimal and correct. All three deviations sound; trust-but-verify pass (read both files end-to-end + independent lint/typecheck/test) confirmed accuracy. Token usage ~285K cumulative (parent + executor agent across 45 tool uses); main-session reasoning stayed lean via targeted Read calls only (no full PRODUCT.md or v2 spec reads needed because 5.1a barrel already abstracts derivation contract). Continues [[feedback_marine_guardian_opus_executor_default]] doctrine — Opus executor on Marine-Guardian remains the right default after this second consecutive clean dispatch.
